course_description:
This course provides students with a foundation in the science and technology of geographical information systems (GIS). GIS science focuses on ways to describe and explain geographical patterns and processes. GIS technology focuses on data modelling, databases and map visualisation. The course provides GIS application skills in fields such as ecology, environmental, marine and earth sciences and physical geography, and provides laboratory sessions for students to apply GIS for practical problem solving in these fields.

This course introduces practicalﾠmethods and concepts for applying geographical information systems (GIS) for mapping and analysing geographical data. The subject considers the use of GIS in the broadly defined areas of geography and environmental science, and provides laboratory sessions for students to apply GIS to practical problem solving.

Lectures are accessible online via Zoom and are recorded. Most lectures have required readings. Practical lab sessions are closely tied to lectures and may include quizzes to bridge the gap between theory and practical application.

Students are able to gain sufficient understanding of course content from online learning materials provided on the Blackboard Learning Management System (LMS). This includes GIS practicals which have guided instructions. Note however, that practical lab sessions include quizzes that are completed in the session.

External courses are delivered entirely online, and students must participate online for learning and assessment.

Note: students may be required to sit exams at a UQ campus or an approved off-campus exam centre. Please refer to the Assessment tab details for information on exam attendance requirements

course_aims:
This course aims to equip students with:

1. The core skills and conceptual understanding required to apply GIS technologies to solve geographical problems

2. An appreciation of the broad range of applications of GIS technologies, including applications in the geographical, environmental and social sciences

learning_outcomes.description: Explain how geographical information is used to answer questions
Describe how spatial data is represented and organised in a GIS
Identify, interpret and apply methods for GIS data capture
Identify, interpret and apply methods for spatial data visualisation using GIS
Identify, interpret and apply methods for spatial data analysis using GIS
Identify, interpret and apply methods for decision-making using GIS
Integrate GIS methods to solve geographical problems
Evaluate GIS applications and approaches to plan and document GIS projects
Develop team building and teamwork skills during Group Work activities

assessment_details.title: In-Lab Quiz
assessment_details.task_description: Short quiz related to 6 of the practical labs. These encourage students to understand the theory and to prepare for GIS lab tasks. The aim is to make efficient use of time and to improve GIS learning. They are marked, and the best 5 out of 6 are used for the assessment grade. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Quiz will be available to do over two days, but once started it has a time limit to complete. No late submission or extensions. Final mark for these assessments will be based on best 5 out of 6.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
Extensions are not available for this assessment as they are open for consecutive days. The best FIVE out of the SIX quizzes will be used to calculate your final grade for this assessment. If you are unable to attend more than one practical due to exceptional circumstances, please contact your course coordinator.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessment_details.title: Practical Exercise 1 on GIS Mapping
assessment_details.task_description: Practical exercises on geospatial concepts and GIS mapping skills to solve a practical problem. This assessment is based on lectures on GIS concepts and practical mapping outputs (weeks 1-4). It is comprised of: i) A GIS map layout to visually assess a resource on UQ campus, i.e. locations for cafes, information directories. ii) Higher-order thinking quiz questions on GIS concepts and principles from lectures and lab practicals, may also include some multiple-choice questions The full requirements for the first task 'GIS map layout' are provided 2 weeks prior to due date. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The requirements for GIS map layout [item i] are typically available 2 weeks prior to due date, and Blackboard submission is opened 1 week prior to due date.
Submitted online through Blackboard. Students need to store documentation for GIS tasks and retain a copy of submission. Refer to Blackboard for the submission link. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 7 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Students will be provided feedback and model answers 1 week after submission; therefore extensions cannot exceed this limit. Please contact your course coordinator if you are impacted longer than 7 days for consideration of an alternate arrangement. You must be able to demonstrate exceptional circumstances for consideration of an alternate assessment.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
You are required to submit assessable items on time. If you fail to meet the submission deadline for any assessment item, then 10% of the maximum possible mark for the assessment item (assessment ‘marked from’ value) will be deducted as a late penalty for every day (or part day) late after the due date. For example, if you submit your assignment 1 hour late, you will be penalised 10%; if your assignment is 24.5 hours late, you will be penalised 20% (because it is late by one 24-hour period plus part of another 24-hour period).

assessment_details.title: Practical Exercise 2 on GIS Analysis
assessment_details.task_description: Practical exercises on geospatial analysis and GIS analysis skills to solve a practical problem. This assessment is based on lecture concepts and practical mapping outputs for weeks 5-8. It is comprised of: i) The GIS risk analysis of flooding for the UQ campus. A report (based on a word template) is submitted which includes text, maps, tables and charts. ii) Higher-order thinking quiz questions on GIS concepts and principles from lectures and lab practicals, and may also include some multiple-choice questions. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. assessment_details.title: GIS Project and Report
assessment_details.task_description: The GIS project is based on a site visited in the field trip to evaluate physical site factors and allocate future land use options. The output is a group report (group size 2-3) where each member focuses on at least one objective and collaborate on overall project and land use allocation. External students will be provided with resources to complete the assessment. A virtual field trip will be available. On project completion, a ‘post-completion’ check will take place. Both the ‘Group project and report’ assessment task and the group peer assessment task (BuddyCheck) will be moderated as part of this check. Based on the outcome of this, a post completion meeting will be arranged that includes an individual question and answer session as a check confirming authorship and project understanding. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submitted online through Blackboard. Students need to store documentation for GIS tasks and retain a copy of submission.
Online submission via Blackboard only by the due date and time. Refer to Blackboard for the submission link. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Students must attend the ‘pre-completion’ meeting at their designated time. Please contact your course coordinator with supporting documentation of the exceptional circumstances that prevented you from attending for consideration of an alternate arrangement.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
You are required to submit assessable items on time. If you fail to meet the submission deadline for any assessment item, then 10% of the maximum possible mark for the assessment item (assessment ‘marked from’ value) will be deducted as a late penalty for every day (or part day) late after the due date. For example, if you submit your assignment 1 hour late, you will be penalised 10%; if your assignment is 24.5 hours late, you will be penalised 20% (because it is late by one 24-hour period plus part of another 24-hour period).

learning_resources.library_resources: Find the required and recommended resources for this course on the UQ Library website.

learning_activities.topic: Geographical data and information Course description and introduction to geographic concepts Practical: Using ArcGIS to make a map of UQ campus Learning outcomes: L01

GIS Data Models and Capture To know the main spatial models for representing geographic data and ways of digitally capture it Practical: Data capture exercise to map a service feature on campus Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L08

Geospatial Data Characteristics and Cartographic Mapping Apply cartographic design principles for effective communication Practical: Mapping demand for a service feature on campus Learning outcomes: L04

Geospatial data as an infrastructure Know how geodetic datums and map projections work to integrate geospatial data Practical: Assistance to complete first practical exercise Learning outcomes: L02, L08
Raster Data Analysis Know how and when to apply raster geoprocessing techniques Practical: Use raster analysis to derive flood areas Learning outcomes: L02, L05
Vector Data Analysis Know how and when to apply vector geoprocessing techniques Practical: Use vector analysis to derive flood impacts Learning outcomes: L02, L05

Geodatabases and Queries Learn two data modelling methods to develop databases and doing data queries Practical: Create database table from flood inspection data and assess tolerable risks Learning outcomes: L02, L05
Mapping from satellite imagery Viewing and manipulation of satellite images in GIS No practical due to field trip Learning outcomes: L05, L07

Day field trip for land use suitability study and stakeholder workshop Day field trip associated with the GIS Project for a land use suitability study. The field trip visits sites and conducts workshops in areas close to the University. Participation is expected; students may request not to attend (given a valid reason) but will be given an alternative activity. Learning outcomes: L03, L08

Making decisions with GIS Know how to transform geospatial data into decision criteria and do additive decision analysis Practical: Building land use suitability maps in GIS Learning outcomes: L06, L07
GIS programming Learn basic programming with Python to do custom tabular calculations Practical: Allocate land uses for objectives Learning outcomes: L03, L05
Consultation on GIS Land Use Project Zoom session open to consultation on GIS land use project Practical: Open session for GIS Project Learning outcomes: L06, L07, L08

Pre-completion Check Group meetings arranged at lecture and practical times that includes individual questions as a threshold check (e.g., confirming authorship and project understanding). These are marked as feedback but do not raise the grade beyond the written submission. Learning outcomes: L04, L06, L08
Industry Talk Discuss course learnings, follow-on study options and GIS application opportunities. No practical
